2606.17810.title: No-Free-Fairness: Fundamental Limits and Trade-offs in Learning Systems
2606.17810.paper_summary: In this paper, we establish a set of theoretical impossibility results, termed the No-Free-Fairness theorems, that identify three fundamental sources of disparity in learning systems. First, we show that when a task exhibits irreducible cost on a subgroup, any decision rule must trade off overall performance with disparity, yielding an inherent fairness--cost frontier. Second, we prove that even in ideal, noise-free settings where a perfectly fair and accurate solution exists, finite-sample learning alone induces nontrivial subgroup disparity, ruling out distribution-free fairness guarantees. More seriously, enforcing strict relative fairness creates a statistical bottleneck: achieving low cost may require exponentially many samples. Third, we show that limitations of the model class can independently induce disparity: if the model cannot represent accurate solutions for a subgroup, fairness remains unattainable regardless of data or training procedure. Overall, these results demonstrate that unfairness is not solely a consequence of biased data or suboptimal optimization, but arises from the intrinsic structure of decision problems, the constraints of finite data, and the expressivity of models. Our framework applies broadly beyond standard supervised learning, and suggests that achieving fairness requires explicit trade-offs and should be treated as a core design consideration.
2606.17810.paper_summary_zh: 在本文中，我們建立了一組理論上不可能的結果，稱為無免費公平性定理，這些結果識別出學習系統中三個基本的不平等來源。<lb/>首先，我們展示了當一個任務在某個子群體上表現出不可減少的成本時，任何決策規則必須在整體性能與不平等之間進行權衡，產生固有的公平性--成本邊界。<lb/>其次，我們證明了即使在理想的無噪聲環境中，存在完美公平且準確的解，僅僅依賴有限樣本學習也會引起非平凡的子群體不平等，排除了無分佈公平性保證。<lb/>更嚴重的是，強制執行嚴格的相對公平性會產生統計瓶頸：達成低成本可能需要指數級的樣本數。<lb/>第三，我們展示了模型類的限制可以獨立引起不平等：如果模型無法為某個子群體表示準確的解，那麼無論數據或訓練過程如何，公平性都無法實現。<lb/>總體而言，這些結果表明，不公平性不僅僅是偏見數據或次優優化的結果，而是源於決策問題的內在結構、有限數據的約束以及模型的表達能力。<lb/>我們的框架廣泛適用於標準監督學習之外，並且表明實現公平性需要明確的權衡，應被視為核心設計考量。
2606.17810.author: Khoat Than et.al.
